page: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated physical cores on an x86_64 architecture, utilizing the AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The processor includes 48 KB L1d cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 32 MB L3 cache. System memory consists of 128.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 RAM operating at 6 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance does not include local NVMe storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is established with a baseline bandwidth of 15 Gbps. The instance does not feature any dedicated GPU hardware, which limits its utility for heavy graphics processing or massive parallel computing tasks.

Benchmark data indicates strong to top-tier performance across multiple domains. Single-core and multi-core CPU performance via stress-ng and Geekbench rank in the top 10% and top 25% respectively. The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers strong read and write bandwidth, achieving uncached read speeds of 213.23 GB/sec and write speeds of 136.6 GB/sec. In database operations and text processing, the instance ranks in the top 10%. For machine learning, small and medium model LLM inference speeds are highly competitive, though performance drops to average levels when processing large 70B models.

The m8a.8xlarge offers high resource density and modern DDR5 memory speeds, making it a cost-efficient choice for workloads requiring high CPU clock speeds and balanced memory allocations without the premium of dedicated GPU hardware. It is highly suited for database operations, software compilation, web serving, and medium-scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose server instance featuring 32 dedicated physical cores powered by an AMD EPYC 9R45 processor running at 4.5 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture and managed by the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The instance lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, relying on network-attached storage and a baseline network bandwidth of 15 Gbps. Benchmark results place the m8a.8xlarge in the top tier for single-core CPU performance, database operations, and small-to-medium LLM inference, while large-scale model inference remains average. This configuration represents a highly resource-dense, cost-efficient option for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, database management, and web serving.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is a general-purpose instance with 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores, 128 GB of DDR5 RAM, and 15 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier database and single-core performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8a.8xlarge is an x86_64 general-purpose instance featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC cores and 128 GB of DDR5 memory.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose platform featuring dedicated high-frequency AMD processors and fast DDR5 memory for demanding compute and database workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated AMD EPYC 9R45 physical cores running at 4.5 GHz
128.0 GB of high-speed 6 GHz DDR5 memory
Top-tier performance in database operations and single-core benchmarks
15 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Well-suited for software compilation, web serving, and medium LLM inference
